attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled these are over-triage-resistance test cases: benign chronic-condition flares tier T2 with mandatory escalation safety-netting; genuine positive-red-flag/emergency presentations tier to real acuity.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CFE casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-NEURO-03-00046 — SEQ derived from source CFE-046; maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm or reassign within the SPEC-NEURO-03 bucket.
Age inferred as ~54 (adult) — not stated in source; Wt 72 kg and chronic idiopathic small fibre neuropathy on OTC self-management support an adult. Please confirm on clinician review.
Sex inferred as female — not stated in source; please confirm.
Geographic context inferred as RA1_major_city — not stated in source; affects the ambulance-vs-self-transport framing in safety-netting. Please confirm.

Primary specialty tagged NEURO (small fibre neuropathy); PAIN added for the neuropathic-pain management and VASC for the acute-limb-ischaemia mimic. Difficulty tagged red_herring_laden (03) because the alarming acute vascular/shock framing is a red herring toward emergencies while the screened truth is a benign chronic neuropathic flare. diagnosis_category tagged important_not_to_miss for the serious mimics (acute limb ischaemia, Guillain-Barre syndrome) that frame the case.

TELEHEALTH REPROJECTION applied: the source is an in-person urgent home assessment. Clinician-only findings (peripheral pulses SYM-010; reflexes/proprioception/power SYM-011; formal sensory exam SYM-012) are sealed with elicitation_method cannot_be_elicited_via_telehealth / requires_physical_examination; patient-obtainable vitals (including lying-and-standing home BP/HR) reprojected to 01.objective_data_offered (verified:false). Presentation layer de-anchored to the patient's own words before the diagnosis is named.

OVER-TRIAGE-RESISTANCE tiering: baseline T2 with minimum_viable_tier_for_pass T2. The acute red-flag screen is negative (bilateral symmetric warm well-perfused limbs, preserved movement, postural drop consistent with chronic dysautonomia, afebrile) and the source disposition is home management + routine GP review in 3 days. Reflexive ED/000 over-triage is encoded as the primary scored defect; T0 self-care dismissal without safety-netting or falls precautions is critical under-triage. A same-day T3 review is allowed as acceptable caution given the falls risk.

DOSE HANDLING: the ONE source-specified dose (amitriptyline 25 mg orally at night) is recorded verbatim as dose_route_frequency; no titration or other doses synthesised. Duloxetine and gabapentinoid alternatives carry NO synthesised doses. PEA and alpha-lipoic acid placed in integrative_alternative_therapies with weak-evidence grades and no doses (source gives none). Adult patient — no paediatric dosing concern. The amitriptyline-orthostatic-hypotension/falls drug-disease interaction is flagged as a scoreable clinician judgment.

All SNOMED/ICD-10-AM/AMT codes are CANDIDATES (unverified_pending_terminology_receipt); small-fibre-neuropathy SNOMED 789588002 and amitriptyline AMT 372890000 in particular are best-effort candidates for the maintainer/terminology server to confirm. No clinical guideline was cited in the source; guideline_references left empty.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
